description: §61.3 — an `<endpoint>` whose `accepts=` resolves to a CONCRETE non-enum type (a struct) fires E-ENDPOINT-ACCEPTS-NOT-ENUM. Unlike §60.5's response-half Info-lint, a non-enum INBOUND accepts= is a HARD Error: parseVariant is a tagged-variant decoder with no recoverable raw path. A non-enum does NOT also fire E-ENDPOINT-NOT-EXHAUSTIVE (no double-report).
rationale: §61.3: `accepts=` SHALL resolve to a :enum type (parseVariant §41.13 cannot decode against a non-enum). A struct is E-ENDPOINT-ACCEPTS-NOT-ENUM (W3 typer) — an Error, NOT the §60.5 Info-lint; and it suppresses the exhaustiveness check (no double-report).
